title: Grapher
description: Transform trajectory and channel analysis tool for Unreal Engine. Visualize and compare motion tracks from CSV files and Sequencer side-by-side.

sections.title: Overview
body: Grapher is an editor plugin for visualizing transform data from CSV files and Sequencer. It is designed for quick spatial and numeric comparison of multiple motion tracks.

Main capabilities: load one or more transform tracks from CSV or Sequencer, visualize trajectories in three projections (X/Y, X/Z, Y/Z), and analyze six dedicated channels - Position X, Y, Z and Pan, Tilt, Roll - using separate charts.

sections.title: Interface
sections.caption: Grapher showing trajectory projections and channel charts

sections.title: Installation and Access
steps: Open Unreal Editor and go to Edit → Plugins.
Search for Grapher, enable it, and restart the editor when prompted.
Open the tool from the top menu: Window → Transform Grapher.

sections.title: Typical Workflow
steps: Import tracks: click Load CSV for file-based data, or Load Sequencer for sequence-derived data.
Use the playback controls to scrub and animate through the timeline.
Inspect trajectory in the three projection panes (X/Y, X/Z, Y/Z).
Inspect the channel charts for Position X/Y/Z and Pan/Tilt/Roll below.
Toggle per-track visibility and use alignment for comparison between takes.

sections.title: UI Reference
body: Top Stats Bar - Loaded Tracks count, Timeline Samples, Frame Range (min/max), and Duration.
Left Sidebar - Load CSV and Load Sequencer import buttons, playback controls, trail length setting, alignment toggle, and the track list with per-track visibility checkboxes.
Main Views - Three spatial projection panes: Top (X/Y), Front (X/Z), Side (Y/Z).
Channel Charts Grid - Six separate line charts rendered below the projections: Position X, Position Y, Position Z, Pan (Yaw), Tilt (Pitch), Roll.
Bottom Transport - Play/Reset buttons, timeline slider, current frame label, and time display.

sections.title: CSV Format Reference
sections.body: Expected columns (case-insensitive): Frame, Time, PosX, PosY, PosZ, RotX, RotY, RotZ, ScaleX, ScaleY, ScaleZ. Load CSV supports selecting multiple files - each file becomes one track. Compatible with KeyFlux exports.

sections.title: Sequencer Import Behavior
body: Load Sequencer attempts to resolve a sequence using several fallbacks: the most recently active open Level Sequence editor asset, then a selected Level Sequence asset in the Content Browser.
Nested subsequences are supported via recursive scan. All transform tracks found are loaded as individual tracks in Grapher.
